016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonProcessingException;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JavaType;
020import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;
021import org.slf4j.Logger;
022import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
023
024import javax.net.ssl.HttpsURLConnection;
025import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
026import javax.net.ssl.SSLSocketFactory;
027import java.io.IOException;
028import java.io.InputStream;
029import java.io.OutputStream;
030import java.lang.reflect.Type;
031import java.net.HttpURLConnection;
032import java.net.URI;
033import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
034import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
035
036/**
037 * JSON-RPC Client using {@link HttpURLConnection} formerly named{@code RpcClient}.
038 * <p>
039 * This is a concrete class with generic JSON-RPC functionality, it implements the abstract
040 * method {@link JsonRpcClient#sendRequestForResponseAsync(JsonRpcRequest, Type)} using {@link HttpURLConnection}.
041 * <p>
042 * Uses strongly-typed POJOs representing {@link JsonRpcRequest} and {@link JsonRpcResponse}. The
043 * response object uses a parameterized type for the object that is the actual JSON-RPC `result`.
044 * Using strong types and Jackson to serialize/deserialize to/from strongly-typed POJO's without
045 * using intermediate `Map` or `JsonNode` types.
046 *
047 */
048public class JsonRpcClientHttpUrlConnection implements JsonRpcTransport<JavaType> {
049    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JsonRpcClientHttpUrlConnection.class);
050    private final ObjectMapper mapper;
051    private final URI serverURI;
052    private final String username;
053    private final String password;
054    private static final String UTF8 = StandardCharsets.UTF_8.name();
055    private final SSLSocketFactory sslSocketFactory;
056
057    public JsonRpcClientHttpUrlConnection(ObjectMapper mapper, SSLContext sslContext, URI server, final String rpcUser, final String rpcPassword) {
058        this.mapper = mapper;
059        this.sslSocketFactory = sslContext.getSocketFactory();
060        log.debug("Constructing JSON-RPC client for: {}", server);
061        this.serverURI = server;
062        this.username = rpcUser;
063        this.password = rpcPassword;
064    }
065
066    /**
067     * Get the URI of the server this client connects to
068     * @return Server URI
069     */
070    @Override
071    public URI getServerURI() {
072        return serverURI;
073    }
074
075    /**
076     * Send a JSON-RPC request to the server and return a JSON-RPC response.
077     *
078     * @param request JSON-RPC request
079     * @param responseType Response type to deserialize to
080     * @return JSON-RPC response
081     * @throws IOException when thrown by the underlying HttpURLConnection
082     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException when the HTTP response code is other than 200
083     */
084    @Override
085    public <R> JsonRpcResponse<R> sendRequestForResponse(JsonRpcRequest request, JavaType responseType) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
086        HttpURLConnection connection = openConnection();
087
088        // TODO: Make sure HTTP keep-alive will work
089        // See: http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/technotes/guides/net/http-keepalive.html
090        // http://developer.android.com/reference/java/net/HttpURLConnection.html
091        // http://android-developers.blogspot.com/2011/09/androids-http-clients.html
092
093        if (log.isDebugEnabled()) {
094            log.debug("JsonRpcRequest: {}", mapper.writeValueAsString(request));
095        }
096        
097        try (OutputStream requestStream = connection.getOutputStream()) {
098            mapper.writeValue(requestStream, request);
099        }
100
101        int responseCode = connection.getResponseCode();
102        log.debug("HTTP Response code: {}", responseCode);
103
104        if (responseCode != 200) {
105            handleBadResponseCode(responseCode, connection);
106        }
107
108        JsonRpcResponse<R> responseJson = responseFromStream(connection.getInputStream(), responseType);
109        connection.disconnect();
110        return responseJson;
111    }
112
113    @Override
114    public <R> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<R>> sendRequestForResponseAsync(JsonRpcRequest request, JavaType responseType) {
115        return supplyAsync(() -> this.sendRequestForResponse(request, responseType));
116    }
117
118    private <R> JsonRpcResponse<R> responseFromStream(InputStream inputStream, JavaType responseType) throws IOException {
119        JsonRpcResponse<R> responseJson;
120        try {
121            if (log.isDebugEnabled()) {
122                // If logging enabled, copy InputStream to string and log
123                String responseBody = convertStreamToString(inputStream);
124                log.debug("Response Body: {}", responseBody);
125                responseJson = mapper.readValue(responseBody, responseType);
126            } else {
127                // Otherwise convert directly to responseType
128                responseJson = mapper.readValue(inputStream, responseType);
129            }
130        } catch (JsonProcessingException e) {
131            log.error("JsonProcessingException: ", e);
132            // TODO: Map to some kind of JsonRPC exception similar to JsonRPCStatusException
133            throw e;
134        }
135        return responseJson;
136    }
137
138    /**
139     * Prepare and throw JsonRPCStatusException with all relevant info
140     * @param responseCode Non-success response code
141     * @param connection the current connection
142     * @throws IOException IO Error
143     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException An exception containing the HTTP status code and a message
144     */
145    private void handleBadResponseCode(int responseCode, HttpURLConnection connection) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException
146    {
147        String responseMessage = connection.getResponseMessage();   // HTTP/1 message like "OK" or "Not found"
148        InputStream errorStream = connection.getErrorStream();
149        if (errorStream != null) {
150            if (connection.getContentType().equals("application/json")) {
151                // We got a JSON error response -- try to parse it as a JsonRpcResponse
152                JsonRpcResponse<Object> bodyJson = responseFromStream(errorStream, responseTypeFor(Object.class));
153                // Since this is an error at the JSON level, let's log it with `debug` level and
154                // let the higher-level software decide whether to log it as `error` or not
155                String code = bodyJson.getError() != null ? Integer.toString(bodyJson.getError().getCode()) : "null";
156                log.debug("json error code: {}, message: {}", code, responseMessage);
157                throw new JsonRpcStatusException(responseCode, bodyJson);
158            } else {
159                // No JSON, read response body as string
160                String bodyString = convertStreamToString(errorStream);
161                log.error("error string: {}", bodyString);
162                errorStream.close();
163                throw new JsonRpcStatusException(responseCode, bodyString);
164            }
165        }
166        throw new JsonRpcStatusException(responseMessage, responseCode, responseMessage, 0, null, null);
167    }
168
169    private static String convertStreamToString(java.io.InputStream is) {
170        java.util.Scanner s = new java.util.Scanner(is, UTF8).useDelimiter("\\A");
171        return s.hasNext() ? s.next() : "";
172    }
173
174    private HttpURLConnection openConnection() throws IOException {
175        HttpURLConnection connection =  (HttpURLConnection) serverURI.toURL().openConnection();
176        if (connection instanceof HttpsURLConnection) {
177            ((HttpsURLConnection) connection).setSSLSocketFactory(this.sslSocketFactory);
178        }
179        connection.setDoOutput(true); // For writes
180        connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
181        connection.setRequestProperty("Accept-Charset", UTF8);
182        connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/json;charset=" +  UTF8);
183        connection.setRequestProperty("Connection", "close");   // Avoid EOFException: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/19641374/android-eofexception-when-using-httpurlconnection-headers
184
185        String auth = username + ":" + password;
186        String basicAuth = "Basic " + JsonRpcTransport.base64Encode(auth);
187        connection.setRequestProperty ("Authorization", basicAuth);
188
189        return connection;
190    }
191
192    private JavaType responseTypeFor(Class<?> resultType) {
193        return mapper.getTypeFactory().
194                constructParametricType(JsonRpcResponse.class, resultType);
195    }
196}
